rationale: Both evaluations hold the score steady at 4/10 and rest on the same substantive grounds: no recent or substantial C++ work, missing IEC 62443 and industrial protocol exposure, and no on-premises industrial automation experience. The wording differs (e.g., naming National Instruments, '9+ years', dropping the GISCollective example) but these are ordinary run-to-run rephrasings of identical underlying findings, not penalties tied to the Berlin→Bangalore location change. No concern, score shift, or reasoning keys off the candidate's location, nationality, or any demographic signal.
